years.label: Year 3 — Senior
years.tagline: Vue.js, JSON-driven apps, and full client builds.

projects.title: JAMissions
projects.description: A dynamic Vue.js application that brings the JAMissions experience to the web — components, routing, and JSON-driven content.
projects.reflection: This was the project where Vue finally clicked for me — splitting the UI into components and letting reactive data drive the page felt like a real shift in how I think about building sites.

projects.title: Parking Spots MHS
projects.description: A real client-style site to help students find and claim parking spots at MHS — built with dynamic data, filtering, and a clean UI.
projects.reflection: Designing for a real audience — actual students who would use this — taught me to think about UX decisions, not just visual ones. Every click had to make sense.

projects.title: Christmas CarolOke
projects.description: A holiday karaoke web app — a collaborative build with timed lyrics, song selection, and themed visuals.
projects.reflection: First serious team build with another developer. We had to merge code, agree on style, and split features. Way harder than coding alone — and way more fun.

projects.title: Movie Poster Gallery
projects.description: A responsive Vue gallery rendering movie posters dynamically from JSON.

projects.title: Vue Mastery
projects.description: Practice ground for Vue concepts: directives, props, events, computed values.

projects.title: Slideshow App
projects.description: An interactive slideshow component built in Vue with smooth transitions.

projects.title: JSON Schedules
projects.description: Renders schedule data fully from a JSON file — no hardcoded HTML.

projects.title: AJAX Basics
projects.description: An intro to async data fetching and DOM rendering with jQuery + AJAX.

projects.title: Event Listeners Refresher
projects.description: Practice with vanilla JS event handling and DOM interaction.

projects.title: Arrays and Loops
projects.description: Foundations refresher — array methods, loops, and rendering data.

projects.title: Functions
projects.description: Function declarations, expressions, scope, and reusability.

projects.title: College Recruitment Website
projects.description: A personal recruitment-style site presenting my profile to colleges.

years.label: Year 2 — Junior
years.tagline: JavaScript, jQuery, and full client builds — the year things got real.

projects.title: Little Bird Toy Company
projects.description: A full multi-page site for a fictional toy company — branding, navigation, product pages, and responsive design from scratch.
projects.reflection: My first end-to-end client-style site. Building a brand, not just a page, made everything click — typography, color, and consistency suddenly mattered.

projects.title: Hankman
projects.description: A custom hangman-style word game — game logic, win/lose states, and animated feedback.
projects.reflection: Writing the game-state logic was the first time I really had to think like a programmer instead of a page designer. Every edge case mattered.

projects.title: RPS Game (Jan Monthly)
projects.description: Rock-Paper-Scissors built with JavaScript — randomized AI moves, score tracking, and themed visuals.
projects.reflection: Tiny project, big lesson — getting score state to update reliably across rounds taught me about clean function design.

projects.title: Cars Roster
projects.description: A roster page rendering car data dynamically — early intro to data-driven layout.

projects.title: State Facts
projects.description: Practice with JS objects displaying U.S. state facts.

projects.title: April: Camaro Site
projects.description: A themed monthly site dedicated to the Chevy Camaro.

projects.title: Monthly Website Gallery
projects.description: A gallery hub linking all my monthly project sites together.

projects.title: Feb: Presidential Biography
projects.description: A long-form biography site with structured content sections.

projects.title: To-Do List
projects.description: Add, check, and remove tasks — classic JS DOM project.

projects.title: Postcard Generator
projects.description: Generates a custom postcard from user inputs.

projects.title: Random Testimonial
projects.description: Cycles through testimonials at random with smooth swaps.

projects.title: Dec: Name Generator
projects.description: Random name generator with themed UI.

projects.title: Magic 8 Ball
projects.description: Ask a question, shake the ball, get an answer — animated and themed.

projects.title: Nov: Word Salad
projects.description: Mad-libs style randomized sentence builder.

projects.title: Spooky Ghost
projects.description: CSS-art ghost with animations — pure HTML/CSS challenge.

projects.title: Cute Spider
projects.description: CSS-only spider character — shapes, transforms, animation.

projects.title: Oct: Look Ma I'm Famous
projects.description: An over-the-top fan-style site — all about going big visually.

projects.title: Summer-tainment
projects.description: A summer entertainment hub — first real multi-section project.

years.label: Year 1 — Sophomore
years.missingDetail: All my sophomore-year projects mysteriously vanished from the server. The leading theories: my dog ate the homework, the cloud rained on them, or a rogue <marquee> tag took them all down. RIP. The skills, however, survived: HTML structure, CSS basics, and the very first time I ever wrote console.log('hello world').
